title: St. Francis Xavier's Cathedral, Kottar
tagline: St. Francis Xavier's Cathedral, Kottar — heritage city of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: St. Francis Xavier's Cathedral, Kottar, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: St. Francis Xavier's Cathedral, Kottar is a much-loved heritage city in Tamil Nadu, known for forts and architecture.
overview.description: St. Francis Xavier's Cathedral, Kottar, also known as St. Xavier Church, is a Roman Catholic Latin Rite shrine in Kottar, Nagercoil, in the Kanyakumari district of Tamil Nadu state, India. While Francis Xavier was doing missionary work in Kottar and its neighbourhood, he averted an invasion of Vadugas with the help of the army of Paravars and Padayatchi nadars of Kottar, at Vadasery, thus protecting the people of the Venad kingdom from that attack which was appreciated by the king, Unni Kerala Varma, who became closer to the priest and befriended him from then on.

topPlaces.name: Vadiveeswaram
topPlaces.description: Vadiveeswaram is a village, now part of the town of Nagercoil, in Kanyakumari district in the southern Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The village was originally an agraharam, or a traditional double line of houses occupied by Brahmins and flanking a temple or pair of temples.

topPlaces.name: Kottar
topPlaces.description: Kottar is a locality and a bazaar area of Nagercoil, Tamil Nadu state, in the southernmost part of Peninsular India. It is the original town around which the city of Nagercoil grew and was an ancient trade centre of both Pandyans and Cheras at various times.

topPlaces.name: Nagercoil
topPlaces.description: Nagercoil, natively spelt as Nāgarkovil (Tamil: [n̪aːhɐɾkoːʋil], "Temple of the Nāgas", or Nagaraja-Temple), is a City and the administrative headquarters of Kanyakumari District in Tamil Nadu state, India. Situated close to the tip of the Indian peninsula, it lies on an undulating terrain between the Western Ghats and the Arabian Sea.

topPlaces.name: Meenakshipuram, Nagercoil
topPlaces.description: Meenakshipuram is a neighbourhood of Nagercoil, in Kanniyakumari district of Tamil Nadu state in the peninsular India. It is located at an altitude of 46 m above the mean sea level with the geographical coordinates of 8°10′57.4″N 77°26′08.6″E (i.e., 8.182611°N, 77.435708°E).

topPlaces.name: South Thamaraikulam
topPlaces.description: South Thamaraikulam is a village in Kanyakumari district, Tamil Nadu, near Nagercoil. It is the site of Thamaraikulam Pathi, one of the five holy places of Ayyavazhi.

topPlaces.name: Chemmuthal
topPlaces.description: Chemmuthal is a historic landmark in the Kanyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu, India. It is located in Vilavancode taluk, in the Killiyoor Panchayat.

topPlaces.name: Nagercoil division
topPlaces.description: Nagercoil division is a revenue division in the Kanyakumari district of Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Bawa Kassim Valiyullah Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Bawa Kassim Valiyullah Mosque, also known as the Bava Kassim Waliyullah Masjid, is a mosque, located in the village of Elankadai, near the town of Nagercoil, in the Kanyakumari district of the state of Tamil Nadu, India.

items.note: Vadiveeswaram is a village, now part of the town of Nagercoil, in Kanyakumari district in the southern Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The village was originally an agraharam, or a traditional double line of houses occupied by Brahmins and flanking a temple or pair of temples.

Kottar is a locality and a bazaar area of Nagercoil, Tamil Nadu state, in the southernmost part of Peninsular India. It is the original town around which the city of Nagercoil grew and was an ancient trade centre of both Pandyans and Cheras at various times.

Nagercoil, natively spelt as Nāgarkovil (Tamil: [n̪aːhɐɾkoːʋil], "Temple of the Nāgas", or Nagaraja-Temple), is a City and the administrative headquarters of Kanyakumari District in Tamil Nadu state, India. Situated close to the tip of the Indian peninsula, it lies on an undulating terrain between the Western Ghats and the Arabian Sea.

Meenakshipuram is a neighbourhood of Nagercoil, in Kanniyakumari district of Tamil Nadu state in the peninsular India. It is located at an altitude of 46 m above the mean sea level with the geographical coordinates of 8°10′57.4″N 77°26′08.6″E (i.e., 8.182611°N, 77.435708°E).
South Thamaraikulam is a village in Kanyakumari district, Tamil Nadu, near Nagercoil. It is the site of Thamaraikulam Pathi, one of the five holy places of Ayyavazhi.
Chemmuthal is a historic landmark in the Kanyakumari district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu, India. It is located in Vilavancode taluk, in the Killiyoor Panchayat.
Nagercoil division is a revenue division in the Kanyakumari district of Tamil Nadu, India.

The Bawa Kassim Valiyullah Mosque, also known as the Bava Kassim Waliyullah Masjid, is a mosque, located in the village of Elankadai, near the town of Nagercoil, in the Kanyakumari district of the state of Tamil Nadu, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Francis Xavier's Cathedral, Kottar?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Francis Xavier's Cathedral, Kottar is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Francis Xavier's Cathedral, Kottar?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (67 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (64 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Francis Xavier's Cathedral, Kottar?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Francis Xavier's Cathedral, Kottar at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Francis Xavier's Cathedral, Kottar?
faq.a: Stays in St. Francis Xavier's Cathedral, Kottar start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Francis Xavier's Cathedral, Kottar?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vadiveeswaram, Kottar, Nagercoil, Meenakshipuram, Nagercoil, South Thamaraikulam.
